title: Tangy Dense Ethiopian Tomatoes Gomen
description: A romantic and flavorful Ethiopian dish featuring chopped tomatoes, namul-blanch-season, and a hint of spice. This whole30-friendly recipe is perfect for a weeknight dinner or special occasion.

ingredients.name: minced garlic
ingredients.notes: microwave for 10 seconds to soften before mincing

instructions: Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add minced onion and cook, stirring occasionally, until softened and lightly browned, about 8 minutes.
Add minced garlic and cook, stirring constantly, for 1-2 minutes or until fragrant.
Add ground ginger, ground cumin, ground coriander, and namul-blanch-season. Cook, stirring constantly, for 1 minute or until spices are fragrant.
Add cherry tomatoes, beef broth, and lemon juice. Bring to a simmer.
Reduce heat to medium-low and cook, stirring occasionally, until tomatoes have broken down and the sauce has thickened, about 15-20 minutes.
Stir in chopped fresh cilantro and season with salt to taste.
Serve hot, garnished with additional cilantro if desired.
Let the dish rest for 5 minutes before serving to allow the flavors to meld together.
Serve over rice, injera, or with crusty bread for a delicious and filling meal.
